fields.name: sound references|ABCDCC!*#Legacy field - please edit in new frame event tag below
effect references|ABCDCC!*#Legacy field - please edit in new frame event tag below
override blend in time{override blend time}
a!
fields.definition:
IMPORTANT NOTES ABOUT FRAME EVENTS
1) The following four fields (hidden except in expert mode) are legacy Halo3-style tag blocks.
2) New frame events (attached to animations in Maya) will automagically be exported to
   the frame_event_list any time a model sidecar is imported with the tool import command.
3) Do not add new frame events, fx events, audio events, or dialog events here.  Instead,
   please use the frame_event_list tag referenced in 'imported events' (it's right above the
   'animations' tag block).
4) The only time you should be editing the following hidden fields is to remove legacy
   frame events that have been replaced by events generated in Maya.

fields.name: frame events|ABCDCC!*#Legacy field - please edit in new frame event tag below
sound events|ABCDCC!*#Legacy field - please edit in new frame event tag below
effect events|ABCDCC!*#Legacy field - please edit in new frame event tag below
dialogue events|ABCDCC!*#Legacy field - please edit in new frame event tag below
interpolation rate:[0,1]#A value of zero or one means no interpolation.
Weight Sources
fields.definition: The primary and secondary weight source will compound if both set. Each will apply individually if the other is not set.

fields.name: primary weight source object function{weight source object function}
weight function#Function applied to input from weight function source
Frame Ratio or Playback Speed
fields.definition: Enter either or leave blank.  Entering both will default to frame ratio option for playback control.

fields.name: intermediate gait name#animation name used for the speed variations
source marker#the marker name on this object where the point of attachment is
destination marker#the marker name of the attachment destination point
ik weight source function#identifies an object function whose value (when evaluated during any animation) will control the weight of this IK point
rank!#calculated during post process where rank is default ordinal for solving
antecedents!#calculated during post process where bit index represents chain index
state name^#name of the state this transition starts in
mode name#name of the mode this transition ends in
state name#name of the state this transition ends in
marker#the marker name on the object being attached
attach to marker#the marker name object (weapon, vehicle, etc.) the above marker is being attached to
animation parameter#Numerical value associated with the ranged action animation (e.g. velocity for jumps)
marker name#this marker should be parented to the vehicle root node
contact marker name#this marker should be parented to the wheel node
mass point offset#distance along the vehicle's up direction to move the wheel from the marker location
Destroyed Suspension
fields.definition: Only Necessary for suspensions with a destroyed state

options: use a single mixing board{unused smelly flag}
do not inherit for player graphs
damage left shoulder{damage focus left shoulder}
damage left arm{damage focus left arm}
damage left leg{damage focus left leg}
damage left foot{damage focus left foot}
damage right shoulder{damage focus right shoulder}
damage right arm{damage focus right arm}
damage right leg{damage focus right leg}
damage right foot{damage focus right foot}
strict looping#loop without blending the first and last frames
